title: Receptionist

rationales.taskAutomation: Call routing, visitor check-in, and basic scheduling are now scripted well enough for AI voice and kiosk systems to handle end-to-end in most settings.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI phone attendants and digital check-in kiosks are mature, commodity products deployed widely across offices and healthcare settings.
rationales.adoption: Mainstream in corporate offices and increasingly common in healthcare and hospitality front desks.
rationales.agenticExposure: Reception is substantially a routing-and-scheduling pipeline an agent can traverse; in-person judgment for security and VIP handling is the remaining human core.

tasksAtRisk: Answering and routing routine phone calls
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4171 (Receptionists and Information Clerks), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI voice attendants and check-in kiosks now handle most routine front-desk traffic, which is why this role sits at High risk. The remaining value is in-person: reading a nervous visitor, handling a security issue, making a VIP feel looked after — things a kiosk can't do.
